# @zakkster/lite-i18n

> Zero-GC reactive internationalization built on @zakkster/lite-signal. Compile
> messages once at defineMessages -- runtime lookup is one Map.get and one fn
> call, allocating nothing but the returned string. Synchronous reactive flush,
> no microtask scheduler. ESM-only, ~3.1 KB min+gz core + ~0.6 KB Format entry,
> single peer dep on @zakkster/lite-signal (^1.4.0), MIT licensed.

The library exposes translation primitives (t, plural, locale, defineMessages),
async loading (loadLocale, ready), fallback chain, missing-key policies, and a
tree-shakeable Intl formatter surface (number, date, list, relative-time) with
both convenience and factory forms. Built for animation loops, Twitch
Extensions, game HUDs, per-tenant SDKs, and SSR (one instance per request).

## Core concepts

- **Message**: a template string. Three internal shapes: static (`"Welcome"`),
  interpolation (`"Hi, {name}"`), or inline plural (`"{count, plural, one
  {# item} other {# items}}"`). Compiled at defineMessages into a closure over
  a stable token array.

- **Dict**: a namespace. Values are strings, plural-object entries
  (`{ one, other, =0, ... }`), or nested dicts. Nested dicts flatten to
  dot-paths at define time so runtime lookup is a single Map.get.

- **Plural-object entry**: an object with an `other` key and only CLDR
  selectors (`zero`, `one`, `two`, `few`, `many`, `other`) or exact patterns
  (`=0`, `=1`, ...) as own-property keys. Detected structurally at define
  time. Missing `other` -> treated as nested dict.

- **Inline plural**: the ICU-lite syntax `{var, plural, sel {sub} sel {sub}}`.
  `#` inside a sub-template is shorthand for `{var}`. Balanced-brace parser
  supports mixing literals, `{slot}`, and one plural block per template
  (nested plurals not supported).

- **Reactive signals**: each instance holds a `_locale` signal (current
  locale) and a `_epoch` signal (bumped on defineMessages for active/fallback
  locales, and on setFallback). `t()` and `plural()` subscribe to both, so
  effects/computeds re-run on any change.

- **Fallback chain**: array of locales walked in order when a key is missing
  in the current locale. The active locale is skipped if listed. Chain flips
  bump the epoch. Defining messages for a locale NOT in `[active, ...chain]`
  does NOT bump the epoch (avoids spurious re-fires for lazy chunks belonging
  to other locales).

- **Missing-key policy**: `"key"` (default -- return literal), `"warn"`
  (console.warn + return literal), `"throw"` (throw `MissingKeyError`).
  Fallback chain walks BEFORE the policy fires. Optional `onMissingKey(key,
  locale)` hook can short-circuit; return a string to use it, return void to
  fall through.

- **Async loader**: `loadLocale(loc, fn)` runs a user-supplied loader, caches
  the dict, and flips `ready(loc)` signal to true. Race-safe: in-flight calls
  share a promise; already-loaded resolves immediately without calling the
  loader; errors clear the in-flight slot so callers can retry.

- **Isolated instance**: `createI18n(config)` creates a fresh reactive world
  with its own signals, dicts, and PluralRules cache. Multiple instances
  share nothing. `setDefaultI18n(inst)` swaps the instance used by top-level
  helpers; `locale` is an ESM live binding so importers see the new signal
  on next reference (destructured captures are stale).

- **Formatters** (`@zakkster/lite-i18n/format`): reactive wrappers around
  `Intl.NumberFormat`, `Intl.DateTimeFormat`, `Intl.ListFormat`,
  `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat`. Two shapes: convenience (`formatNumber(v, opts,
  i18n?)`) with WeakMap(opts)-keyed cache; factory (`numberFormat(opts,
  i18n?)`) returning a per-locale-cached `(value) => string`. Convenience is
  zero-alloc for hoisted opts; factory is zero-alloc unconditionally after
  the first call per locale.

## Architecture invariants

- **Compile once, reuse forever**: `defineMessages` walks the dict, flattens
  nested paths, tokenizes each template, and compiles a closure per entry.
  The closure signature is `(params, locale, getPluralRules) => string`.
  Compiled entries stored in `Map<locale, Map<key, fn>>`.

- **Uniform call site**: every compiled entry -- static, simple, plural-obj,
  inline-plural -- has the same signature. Monomorphic invocation at the
  lookup site is critical for V8 IC performance.

- **Token types**: 0 = literal (`{ str }`), 1 = slot (`{ key }`), 2 = plural
  block (`{ variable, exact: Map<number, tokens>, variants: Map<selector,
  tokens> }`). Sub-templates inside plural variants use only types 0 and 1;
  `#` compiles to `{ type: 1, key: <plural variable> }`.

- **PluralRules cache**: `Map<locale, Intl.PluralRules>` per instance. Lazy;
  one instance per unique locale used with a plural.

- **32-bit modular epoch**: `_epoch` is bumped via `(n + 1) | 0`, wrapping
  signed. lite-signal handles the modular comparison; the counter never
  overflows.

- **String allocation is the only per-call allocation**: the token loop
  builds the result via `out += ...`. No intermediate arrays or objects. No
  regex per call.

- **Reactivity is synchronous**: `_epoch.set(v)` -> `t()` calls in dependent
  effects flush in the same call stack. Same discipline as lite-signal.

## Performance characteristics

- `t()` with static template -- O(1), zero alloc (except the returned string).
- `t()` with N slots -- O(N), zero alloc.
- `t()` with inline plural -- O(N + P) where P is size of the winning variant.
- `plural(key, count, params?)` -- one small merge alloc for `{...params,
  count}`. For hot loops, use `t(key, params)` with `count` in `params`.
- `formatNumber(v, opts)` (hoisted opts) -- one Map.get + Intl format call.
- `numberFormat(opts)()` (factory) -- one Map.get + Intl format call. Same,
  no WeakMap layer.
- `defineMessages` -- O(entries * template length). Allocation-heavy; not on
  hot path.
- `setFallback` / `defineMessages` for active or fallback locale -- bump
  epoch, O(observers). Standard signal cost.
- `loadLocale` -- O(1) + Promise infrastructure (unavoidable). Not for hot
  paths.

## Version notes

- **1.0.0** (initial). Core surface: signal (`locale`, `_epoch`, `ready`),
  translation (`t`, `plural`), definition (`defineMessages`), async loading
  (`loadLocale`, `ready`), fallback chain (`setFallback`), missing-key
  policies (`setMissingKeyPolicy`, `onMissingKey`), instance isolation
  (`createI18n`, `setDefaultI18n`, `getDefaultI18n`), diagnostics (`stats`),
  errors (`MissingKeyError`). Format entry: four convenience formatters,
  four factory formatters, `createFormatters(i18n)` bulk binder. 76 tests
  (72 behavior + 4 zero-GC under --expose-gc), 0 failures. Retained heap
  ceilings: 100k t() calls with interpolation < 200 KB; 100k inline plural
  calls < 300 KB; 100k factory numberFormat calls < 100 KB; 1000 redefine
  cycles < 500 KB.

## When to use

- Twitch Extensions with locale-configurable UIs.
- Game HUDs, scoreboards, telemetry overlays with i18n.
- Long-running browser sessions where every KB of transient garbage matters.
- Multi-tenant SDKs where each tenant has its own locale + dict.
- SSR (one instance per request via createI18n).
- Anywhere `i18next` / `formatjs` bundle size or GC pressure is a problem.

## When NOT to use

- If you need full ICU MessageFormat with `select`, `selectordinal`, or
  nested plurals -- use `@formatjs/intl-messageformat`.
- If your translations require gender-marking or complex morphological
  agreement -- CLDR plural rules and `select` don't cover this.
- If you need translation-management-system integration (Crowdin, Lokalise,
  Transifex import formats) at runtime -- do the format conversion at build
  time and feed plain dicts.
- If you don't want a peer dep on lite-signal -- the reactivity is the core
  value proposition of this library.

## API summary

```ts
// Core (@zakkster/lite-i18n)

interface I18nConfig {
    locale?:              string;
    fallback?:            string | string[];
    missingKeyPolicy?:    "key" | "warn" | "throw";
    onMissingKey?:        (key: string, locale: string) => string | void;
    messages?:            Record<string, MessageDict>;
}

interface I18n {
    readonly locale: Signal<string>;
    t(key: string, params?: MessageParams): string;
    plural(key: string, count: number, params?: MessageParams): string;
    defineMessages(locale: string, dict: MessageDict): void;
    loadLocale(locale: string, loader: () => Promise<MessageDict>): Promise<void>;
    ready(locale: string): Signal<boolean>;
    setFallback(chain: string | string[]): void;
    setMissingKeyPolicy(p: "key" | "warn" | "throw"): void;
    onMissingKey(fn: ((key: string, locale: string) => string | void) | null): void;
    stats(): I18nStats;
}

function createI18n(config?: I18nConfig): I18n;
function setDefaultI18n(inst: I18n): void;
function getDefaultI18n(): I18n;

// Top-level helpers (default instance)
let locale: Signal<string>;               // ESM live binding
function t(key: string, params?: MessageParams): string;
function plural(key: string, count: number, params?: MessageParams): string;
function defineMessages(locale: string, dict: MessageDict): void;
function loadLocale(locale: string, loader: () => Promise<MessageDict>): Promise<void>;
function ready(locale: string): Signal<boolean>;
function setFallback(chain: string | string[]): void;
function setMissingKeyPolicy(p: "key" | "warn" | "throw"): void;
function onMissingKey(fn: ((key: string, locale: string) => string | void) | null): void;
function stats(): I18nStats;

class MissingKeyError extends Error {
    key: string;
    locale: string;
}

// Format (@zakkster/lite-i18n/format)

// Convenience (WeakMap(opts) -> Map<locale, Intl> cache; hoist opts for zero-alloc)
function formatNumber      (v: number|bigint, opts?, i18n?): string;
function formatDate        (v: Date|number,   opts?, i18n?): string;
function formatList        (items,             opts?, i18n?): string;
function formatRelativeTime(v: number, unit,   opts?, i18n?): string;

// Factory (per-locale cache local to the returned fn; unconditional zero-alloc steady state)
function numberFormat      (opts?, i18n?): (v: number|bigint) => string;
function dateFormat        (opts?, i18n?): (v: Date|number)   => string;
function listFormat        (opts?, i18n?): (items)             => string;
function relativeTimeFormat(opts?, i18n?): (v: number, unit)   => string;

function createFormatters(i18n: I18n): BoundFormatters;  // all 8 bound to instance
```

## Message template DSL

```
Template            := (Literal | Slot | PluralBlock | Quoted)*
Literal             := any character except '{' or "'"; # is literal here (not a slot)
Slot                := '{' Identifier '}'
PluralBlock         := '{' Identifier ',' 'plural' ',' Variant+ '}'
Variant             := Selector '{' SubTemplate '}'
Selector            := CldrSelector | '=' Digits
CldrSelector        := 'zero' | 'one' | 'two' | 'few' | 'many' | 'other'
SubTemplate         := (SubLiteral | Slot | Hash | Quoted)*
SubLiteral          := any character except '{', '#', or "'"
Hash                := '#'   ; equivalent to '{' <plural variable> '}'
Quoted              := "'" QuotedContent "'"
                       ; Only opens when the "'" is followed by '{' '}' or '#'
                       ; Inside the section, '' is a literal apostrophe.
                       ; Outside a section, '' is also a literal apostrophe.
                       ; A "'" not followed by a special char is literal.
Identifier          := [a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*
```

Constraints enforced at compile time:
- Every plural block must contain an `other` variant. Missing -> `SyntaxError`.
- Braces must balance (respecting quoted sections). Unmatched `{` -> `SyntaxError`.
- One plural block per template; nested plurals not supported.
- Slot keys are bare identifiers. A slot with a comma inside (`{n, number}`,
  `{d, date, short}`, `{g, select, ...}`) is any unsupported ICU shape and
  throws `SyntaxError` at define time -- previously these rendered silently
  as `""`.
- Plural selectors must be one of `zero one two few many other` or `=N`. Any
  other selector (typo or unsupported ICU form) throws `SyntaxError`.

Runtime coercion:
- Nullish (`undefined` or `null`) slot values render as `""` (nullish coalesce
  on the hot path). Numeric zero and empty string render normally.
- Slot reads use `Object.hasOwn`, so `{constructor}`, `{__proto__}`, and
  other prototype-chain identifiers render as `""` unless supplied as own
  properties on `params`.
- `defineMessages` is atomic: templates compile into a staging Map first,
  committed only on success. A bad template mid-dict throws without leaving
  a partial update.
- `Intl.PluralRules` construction failure (invalid locale tag) logs a single
  `console.warn` and falls back to the environment default. Cached per locale
  so it can't spam.
- `loadLocale` invokes its loader via `Promise.resolve().then(loaderFn)`, so
  synchronous throws inside the loader route through the async rejection
  path. The inflight slot clears on both success and failure -- retry works
  in every failure mode.

Plural-object entries always use `count` as the variable name (matches
TMS-export conventions and the `plural(key, count, params?)` sugar). For
custom variable names, use the inline ICU form.

Format-factory instance capture: `numberFormat(opts)`, `dateFormat(opts)`,
etc. bind the target i18n instance at factory-creation time (not per call).
If you hoist a factory at module scope and later swap the default via
`setDefaultI18n`, the factory keeps reading the original. For per-tenant or
SSR flows, pass the instance explicitly (`numberFormat(opts, tenantI18n)`)
or use `createFormatters(i18n)`. The convenience form (`formatNumber(v,
opts)`) re-resolves the default each call and does follow swaps.

## Common idioms

```js
// Reactive translation binding
import { effect } from "@zakkster/lite-signal";
import { t, locale } from "@zakkster/lite-i18n";

effect(() => (headerEl.textContent = t("header.title")));
locale.set("bg");  // headerEl updates synchronously

// Plural helper form
defineMessages("en", { items: { one: "# item", other: "# items" } });
plural("items", 3);                       // -> "3 items"

// Inline plural form
defineMessages("en", { m: "{n, plural, one {only #} other {# in total}}" });
t("m", { n: 42 });                        // -> "42 in total"

// Async chunk load with ready gate
import { loadLocale, ready, locale } from "@zakkster/lite-i18n";
effect(() => {
    if (!ready("bg")()) { spinner.show(); return; }
    spinner.hide();
});
loadLocale("bg", () => import("./locales/bg.js").then(m => m.default));

// Fallback chain
const i = createI18n({ locale: "de-CH", fallback: ["de", "en"] });

// Multi-tenant sandbox
const tenants = new Map();
function forTenant(id) {
    let t = tenants.get(id);
    if (!t) {
        t = createI18n({ locale: dbGetLocale(id), messages: dbGetMessages(id) });
        tenants.set(id, t);
    }
    return t;
}

// Zero-alloc formatter for a render loop
import { numberFormat } from "@zakkster/lite-i18n/format";
const scoreFmt = numberFormat({ notation: "compact" });
effect(() => scoreEl.textContent = scoreFmt(score()));
```

## Benchmark snapshot

Zero-GC verification harness (Node 22, --expose-gc, warmed to 10k iterations
then measured over 100k):

| Workload                                                   | Retained heap |
| ---------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| 100k `t()` calls with `{name}` + `{count}` interpolation   | < 200 KB      |
| 100k inline-plural `t()` calls (`{n, plural, ...}`)        | < 300 KB      |
| 100k `numberFormat` factory calls (currency, steady-state) | < 100 KB      |
| 1000 redefine-then-read cycles                             | < 500 KB      |

The only unavoidable per-call allocation is the produced string.

## File layout

- `I18n.js` -- core: tokenizer, compiler, createI18n, top-level default-instance routing.
- `Format.js` -- Intl formatters, factories, createFormatters bulk binder.
- `I18n.d.ts` / `Format.d.ts` -- TypeScript declarations.
- `test/01-core.test.mjs` -- static/interpolation, nested dicts, missing-key policies, isolation.
- `test/02-plural.test.mjs` -- plural-object entries, inline ICU, exact matches, CLDR selectors across en/bg/pl/ar.
- `test/03-fallback.test.mjs` -- chain walk order, epoch bump semantics, non-fallback locale doesn't re-fire effects.
- `test/04-async.test.mjs` -- loadLocale, ready signal, in-flight dedup, error retry.
- `test/05-format.test.mjs` -- all 4 formatters, factory vs convenience, locale-switch reactivity, createFormatters.
- `test/06-zero-gc.test.mjs` -- heap retention checks (--expose-gc).

## Install

```
npm install @zakkster/lite-i18n
```

MIT
